title: First Round of U.S.-Iran Talks Ends With High Hopes and Big Challenges
summary: Mediators reported progress toward reaching a final deal within 60 days. They also said that negotiators had dwelled on issues that were supposed to be settled.

title: Israel Holds to Lebanon Truce, With Troops Kept on Defense
summary: Israel and Hezbollah, the Iran-backed Lebanese militant group, appeared to maintain a tense cease-fire for a second day. Israel’s military has new orders that restrict troops to defensive actions.

title: U.S. Oil Is Skipping the Chance to Grab Market Share From the Gulf
summary: U.S. oil production is expected to grow only modestly next year as companies hesitate to spend more in an uncertain market.

title: Who Is Andy Burnham, the Man Who Could Be Britain’s Next Prime Minister?
summary: Charismatic, northern and exuding a relaxed optimism, Mr. Burnham is a contrast to Keir Starmer. His allies hope he could mend Labour’s relationship with voters.

title: Supreme Court Restores Conviction in 1979 Murder of Etan Patz
summary: Pedro Hernandez was convicted in 2017 of kidnapping and murdering the 6-year-old boy, but an appeals court in July had said Mr. Hernandez was entitled to a new trial.

title: Why Older People Are Divorcing More Than They Used To
summary: Rates of gray divorce have risen sharply over the past few decades — and experts have a few theories as to why.

title: Alan Greenspan, Fed Chairman Through Prosperity and Crisis, Dies at 100
summary: The pre-eminent economic policymaker of his time and a skilled political operator, he favored market-friendly stances that would later come to be associated with destructive financial forces.

title: Clive Davis, Music Industry Titan Who Signed Whitney Houston, Dies at 94
summary: He rose from a midlevel position at Columbia Records to become one of music’s most powerful executives, shepherding stars like Barry Manilow and Whitney Houston.

title: Congressional Democrats Warn Pulte Against Mass Firings
summary: Current and former officials say the acting director of national intelligence is planning to announce major cuts to his office as early as Monday.

title: ‘The View’ Asks Its Audience for Help in Battle With F.C.C.
summary: The daytime TV talk show is at the center of a dispute with the Federal Communications Commission over political speech.

title: He Provided Security to 3 Governors. Then He Snapped.
summary: Lt. Ricardo Santos of the New Jersey State Police had faced questions about his judgment and conduct before he became the primary suspect in a double murder-suicide.

title: How Will Colombia’s New President Fuel Trump’s Drug War?
summary: Abelardo De La Espriella, who has vowed to destroy traffickers with military power, appears set to be the country’s next president.

title: Abelardo De La Espriella, Right-Wing Outsider, Could Be Colombia’s Next President
summary: President Trump endorsed Abelardo De La Espriella, the conservative presidential candidate and apparent winner of Sunday’s election who has strong U.S. ties.

title: Thousands Are Fleeing as Putin Bombs the Donbas Cities He Most Covets
summary: While Kyiv’s fortunes have brightened in other ways in the war, Moscow’s forces are raining bombs and drones on “fortress belt” cities like Sloviansk and Kramatorsk.

title: Ukraine Escalates Crimea Attacks, Creating a New Pressure Point for Russia
summary: To pressure Moscow, Ukraine is stepping up an air campaign to isolate the peninsula, which Russia annexed in 2014.

title: For Schlossberg, Quirky Charm and a Claim to Camelot May Not Be Enough
summary: Jack Schlossberg, the grandson of John F. Kennedy, has found it difficult to overcome some of his perceived shortcomings in a high-profile House primary race.

title: In Utah House Race, Progressives Assail Democrat Who Backed Abortion Restrictions
summary: Ben McAdams, a moderate former congressman, faces a handful of left-wing opponents in a rare opportunity for a Democrat to win in a Republican state.

title: China Tightens Rare-Earth Grip on U.S. Firms, Threatening Trade Clash
summary: The move targets two U.S. manufacturers at the center of the Trump administration’s effort to rebuild the domestic supply chain for critical magnets.

title: Is China Closing the A.I. Gap Faster Than Expected?
summary: Silicon Valley and corporate America are increasingly turning to cheaper, open-source artificial intelligence models built in China.

title: What I Learned About Masculinity at Thai Kickboxing School
summary: A week inside a grueling Muay Thai training program in Phuket.

title: Ten Years After Brexit, the Dismal Verdict Is In
summary: Brexit was supposed to let Britain return to a time when it still counted as a global power. A decade later, the costs are blindingly apparent.

title: The Secret Reason Bosses Want Everyone Back in the Office, Every Day of the Week
summary: It’s all about them.

title: Behind the Wheel
summary: We look into the dangers posed by large S.U.V.s and pickup trucks.

title: A Loophole Brings Cystic Fibrosis Patients a ‘Miracle Drug’ in Generic Form
summary: A generic version of a breakthrough cystic fibrosis drug, manufactured in Bangladesh for a fraction of the American price, may give some families around the world an unlikely lifeline.
